name: Emtricitabine / Tenofovir Alafenamide
summary: Approved antiretroviral combination being evaluated as a reverse-transcriptase-inhibitor geroscience strategy through post hoc biological-age biomarker analysis and MCI/Alzheimer disease trial-watch records.
mechanism_summary: Combines emtricitabine with the tenofovir alafenamide prodrug, producing intracellular nucleotide analog exposure that may inhibit reverse-transcription biology relevant to retroelements while retaining approved antiviral indications outside aging.

open_questions: Whether the DNA methylation aging signal replicates in prospective placebo-controlled geroscience trials
Whether FTC/TAF reduces direct LINE1, retrotransposon cDNA, or interferon target-engagement biomarkers in aging-relevant tissues
Whether effects generalize from young healthy adults to older adults or neurodegeneration populations
